---
title: "Getting Started"
sidebar: en
---

## Requirements

- An iOS, Android or desktop device (macOS, Linux, Windows).
- No server, no account, no sign-in — the runtimes live locally.

## Setup

1. **Install.** Établi Atelier is under development. Download the current **development build (signed APK)** from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/etabli-dev/etabli-atelier/releases) and install it on Android, or build for desktop from the repo. App Store, Google Play and F-Droid releases are not available yet.
2. **Open.** On first launch the app unpacks the bundled WebAssembly runtimes (WebR and Pyodide) into the app-support directory — a one-off, a few seconds depending on device.
3. **Pick a kernel.** Use the toggle at the top of the screen to switch between R and Python.
4. **Start working.** Write scripts, load packages, render plots — all inside the Atelier workspace.

## A typical session

1. In **Files**, create a new `.R` or `.py` file (or open an existing one).
2. In **Editor**, type code and press **Run**.
3. Output (text, table, plot) appears in **Console**; plots also land in **Plots**.
4. Use the share sheet to export a plot as PNG, or the script as a file.

## Notes

- Both kernels share the same virtual filesystem — a CSV written from R is read by Python without any conversion step.
- WebR and Pyodide are open-source WebAssembly distributions, vendored for reproducible builds. Versions and source URLs are documented in `THIRD_PARTY.md` in the repo.
- The first Python launch can take a bit longer while the scientific libraries initialise; subsequent launches load from cache.
